claims.text: President Eisenhower briefly vanished during his Palm Springs vacation on the night of February 20th, 1954.

evidence.quote:
On the night of February 20th, 1954, the
president of the United States vanished
during his Palm Springs vacation.

claims.rationale: The Eisenhower 'missing evening' of Feb 20, 1954 is a documented incident - the Associated Press did briefly report his death before retracting. The factual core (unexplained evening off-itinerary, AP retraction) is historically attested; the paranormal interpretation is a separate downstream claim.

claims.text: The official White House explanation that Eisenhower had emergency dental work in Palm Springs was a cover story.

evidence.quote:
He chipped a tooth and was rushed
to a local dentist

The Eisenhower Library has
extensive records of the president's
health. Every appointment, every
procedure, but there's no record of any
dental work in February 1954

claims.rationale: The Eisenhower Library's medical records genuinely contain no February 1954 dental entry - this is the widely-cited factual anchor of the whole story. However, the absence of a minor-procedure record is weak proof of a cover-up versus poor recordkeeping for a trivial tooth chip. Medium directTruth to reflect real evidentiary gap without overclaiming conspiracy.

claims.text: Eisenhower was actually at Edwards Air Force Base on February 20th, 1954 for a classified meeting with extraterrestrials.

evidence.quote:
He was
at Edward's Air Force Base for the most
classified meeting in American history

claims.rationale: No physical evidence - no photos, no logs, no credible first-hand testimony - supports an Eisenhower-alien meeting. The story originates from Gerald Light's 1954 letter and later whistleblower claims, all of which are unverifiable. Near-zero directTruth because this is extraordinary claim without extraordinary evidence.

dependencies.rationale: If the dental story were true, there would be no window for a Edwards AFB meeting.

claims.text: At the 1954 Edwards meeting, tall pale blonde humanoid 'Nordic' aliens offered humanity free energy, 300-year lifespans, and a golden age in exchange for dismantling all nuclear weapons.

evidence.quote:
All they
wanted was all nuclear weapons
dismantled

claims.rationale: The 'Nordic' Pleiadean contact archetype is a well-known New Age / contactee mythos going back to George Adamski in the 1950s - tall blonde beings offering peace. The specific Eisenhower-Nordic exchange has no documentary support beyond Gerald Light's letter and later copycat accounts. Near-zero directTruth.

dependencies.rationale: Describes the content of the alleged Edwards meeting.

claims.text: Eisenhower rejected the Nordic offer and instead signed a secret treaty with the Grays exchanging advanced military technology for permission to abduct humans.

evidence.quote:
For
Eisenhower, the choice was clear. He
chose power

claims.rationale: The Grada/Grata Treaty is a core UFO conspiracy narrative with no documentary artifacts ever produced. Both of its primary sources (William Cooper, Phil Schneider) were later-life contactee figures whose claims lack corroboration. Near-zero directTruth.

dependencies.rationale: [alternative] The Gray-treaty narrative is the competing path to the Nordic-peace offer - humanity rejected one in choosing the other.

claims.text: A secret cabal called Majestic 12, composed of high-ranking generals and top scientists, coordinates the US government's alien program.

evidence.quote:
This group
would later get a code name, Majestic
12

claims.rationale: The Majestic 12 documents surfaced in 1984 and have been extensively analyzed - the FBI formally concluded in 1988 that they were 'bogus'. No authentic government record has ever established the existence of an MJ-12 body. Low directTruth because multiple independent document-forensics analyses reject the papers.

claims.text: Dulce, New Mexico hosts a secret seven-level underground joint human-alien base where grays keep humans in biological incubation tanks.

evidence.quote:
In the mountains
near Dulce, New Mexico, construction
began on what would become the most
classified facility on Earth

claims.rationale: The Dulce Base legend originates almost entirely with Phil Schneider's public lectures in the 1990s. No satellite imagery, no leaked blueprints, no independent physical access, and no government records support its existence. Ground-penetrating radar studies of the area have been negative. Near-zero directTruth.

claims.text: Geologist Phil Schneider, credentialed structural engineer with security clearance, helped build US government underground bases.

evidence.quote:
Phil Schneider wasn't a conspiracy
theorist. He was a geologist and
structural engineer with security
clearance

In 1979, Schneider was at Dulce, New
Mexico

claims.rationale: Schneider presented himself as a credentialed engineer, but independent investigation has found no documentation of his geology degree, security clearance, or government employment at Dulce. His brother publicly disputed many of his claims after his death. Low directTruth because credentials cited were never substantiated.

claims.text: In 1979 at Dulce, a firefight between US military and Grays killed 66 humans, with Schneider as one of only three survivors.

evidence.quote:
In the battle, 66 humans died.
Only three survived

claims.rationale: A 1979 firefight killing 66 American personnel would have left records - next-of-kin notifications, military casualty data, news reports. None exist. The entire story traces to Schneider's lecture circuit. Near-zero directTruth.

dependencies.rationale: Relies on Schneider's claimed presence at Dulce.

claims.text: William Cooper predicted on June 28, 2001 that a false-flag terrorist attack blamed on Osama bin Laden would strike the US, six weeks before 9/11.

evidence.quote:
On June 28th, 2001 on his
radio show, Cooper said there would be a
major terrorist attack on US soil

claims.rationale: The June 28 2001 broadcast is preserved on archived copies of The Hour of the Time and Cooper did discuss a planned false-flag attack blaming bin Laden. However, bin Laden was already on FBI Most Wanted (since 1999 for the embassy bombings) and was widely discussed in counter-terror circles, so the 'nobody had heard of him' framing overstates prescience. Medium directTruth: broadcast real, implied miraculous foreknowledge overstated.

claims.text: Both whistleblowers Phil Schneider and William Cooper were murdered for exposing the Grata Treaty, with official causes (suicide, shootout) fabricated to hide it.

evidence.quote:
A few weeks later, he
was killed in a shootout with police

claims.rationale: Both deaths are factually real. Cooper was killed in a November 2001 Apache County shootout over a tax-evasion warrant, where he shot a deputy first. Schneider's January 1996 death was ruled suicide (piano-wire asphyxiation) - unusual method, but his family accepts suicide. Attribution to 'alien cover-up assassination' has no evidence; low directTruth reflects suspicious-but-plausible natural explanations.

claims.text: The Brookings Report is a classified document that confirms aliens exist and ordered NASA to suppress the information.

evidence.quote:
Now, you might have heard this is a
classified report that claims aliens
exist and instructed NASA to suppress
this information. None of that is true

claims.rationale: The host explicitly denies this popular claim. The actual 1960 Brookings Report (Proposed Studies on the Implications of Peaceful Space Activities for Human Affairs) is a public document; it discusses the potential social consequences of discovering alien life as a hypothetical, and never claims aliens exist or instructs suppression. Near-zero directTruth.

claims.text: Eisenhower's 1961 farewell-address warning about the military-industrial complex was a coded confession about the alien technology deal he regretted.

evidence.quote:
He warned of the growing
unchecked power of a military industrial
complex

claims.rationale: The Jan 17 1961 farewell address and its MIC warning are real and historically important. The coded-confession reading imposes a UFO-conspiracy frame on what is broadly understood as concern about postwar defense-contractor political power - an interpretation Eisenhower himself explicated in documented correspondence. Low directTruth for the UFO reinterpretation.

claims.text: The choice between Nordic peace and Gray power is a valid parable about human nature even if the literal alien story is unverified.

evidence.quote:
You both have guns
pointed at each other

claims.rationale: Host's own verdict-section framing: even if the story is unprovable, he argues the game-theoretic dilemma (unilateral disarmament requires trust you can't verify) maps onto Cold War decisions humans actually made. This is an opinion/interpretation, so directTruth is omitted rather than guessed.
